sourceMovie.title: The Gentlemen
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Gentlemen (2020) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Guy Ritchie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pulp Fiction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Gentlemen clicked for you, Pulp Fiction (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Gentlemen?
faq.answer: Try Pulp Fiction, Smokin' Aces, Bandits—each shares DNA with The Gentlemen while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Gentlemen?
faq.answer: The Gentlemen (2020) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Gentlemen worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
